stories.title: Opinion | ‘China shock 3.0’ is coming. And it’ll be AI-powered robots
stories.simple_summary: An opinion piece warns that China will soon use AI-powered robots to compete with other countries in manufacturing and business, similar to how China became a major factory for the world decades ago.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly about international economics and what investors should watch for — not something you need to do anything about right now.

stories.title: New Google commercial imagines a Declaration of Independence written with help from AI
stories.simple_summary: Google made an advertisement imagining what the Founding Fathers could have written if they'd had access to Google's AI writing tools.
stories.why_it_matters: This is just marketing — Google is showing off its AI tools, but it doesn't affect how you run your business or life.

stories.title: The Artificial Intelligence (AI) Memory Supercycle Is Getting Stronger. Here's How You Can Profit From This Boom With Less Than $100
AI security questions loom over NATO summit
stories.simple_summary: Military and government leaders meeting at NATO are discussing security concerns about artificial intelligence.
stories.why_it_matters: This is about high-level government policy — nothing specific you need to act on.

stories.title: Midjourney wants Hollywood studios to reveal the details of their AI usage
stories.simple_summary: A company called Midjourney (which makes AI art tools) is demanding that Hollywood studios tell them how those studios use AI in their own work, as part of a legal fight.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly interesting to lawyers and entertainment companies — it doesn't directly affect most small business owners.

stories.title: Alibaba reportedly bans employees from using Claude Code
stories.simple_summary: The Chinese company Alibaba told its employees they cannot use a tool called Claude Code because it considers it too risky.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a corporate policy at one big company — it doesn't affect your business unless you work there.

stories.title: What is Mistral AI? Everything to know about the OpenAI competitor
stories.simple_summary: A company called Mistral AI is building AI tools and has raised a lot of money, with the goal of making advanced AI available to more people instead of keeping it exclusive.
stories.why_it_matters: This means more AI options may become available to small business owners in the future, but nothing is changing right now.

stories.title: For one small business, AI was key to a quick start and expansion
stories.simple_summary: A small business used AI tools to get started quickly and grow faster than it might have otherwise.
stories.why_it_matters: This shows that AI can genuinely help small businesses save time and money — worth paying attention to if you're looking to streamline your work.

stories.title: The fanfiction community is at war with AI — and itself
stories.simple_summary: Writers of fanfiction (stories based on existing movies or books) are fighting with each other about whether anyone should use AI to write stories, and some are trying to catch and punish AI use.
stories.why_it_matters: Unless you write fanfiction online, this doesn't affect you — it's a community debate among creative writers.

stories.title: ‘Who Should I Vote for?’ Voters Turn to A.I. Before Casting Their Ballots
stories.simple_summary: Some voters are asking AI tools like ChatGPT to help them decide who to vote for in elections.
stories.why_it_matters: It's worth knowing that some people are using AI for voting advice, but you should think for yourself about candidates — an AI doesn't know your local issues.

stories.title: The only AI glossary you’ll need this year
stories.simple_summary: TechCrunch published a dictionary explaining new AI words and phrases that have become common.
stories.why_it_matters: Not urgent — but if you're curious about what AI terms mean when you hear them, this could be useful reference material.

stories.title: The browser wars aren’t about search anymore — here are the best alternatives to Chrome and Safari
stories.simple_summary: An article reviews alternative web browsers (the programs you use to visit websites) that people can use instead of Chrome and Safari.
stories.why_it_matters: This is about picking a web browser — mostly a personal choice that doesn't require urgent action.
